DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF THELMA BRUNN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS.PLAINTIFF THELMA BRUNN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT CATHAY MORTUARY-(WAH SANG) IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ITS PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE (PURSUANT TO THE CATEGORIES STATED IN PLAINTIFF'S JANUARY 29, 2018 AMENDED DEPO NOTICE FOR PMK AT CATHAN MORTUARY) ON JUNE 5, 2018 AT 1:30 AT THE LAW OFFICES OF BRUCE S. OSTERMAN, 2300 CONTRA COSTA BLVD., SUITE 320, PLEASANT HILL, CA. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1500 ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT CATHAY MORTUARY - (WAY SANG) IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF BRUNN. SANCTIONS ARE PAYABLE ON OR BEFORE JUNE 5, 2018.PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO JUDGE PRO TEM PETER CATALANOTTI. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Continued To Nov-19-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David May, Esq. 415-399-3900 of the Law Offices of Bruce Osterman for Plaintiff.
Lynne Bentley, Esq. 714-371-2313 of the Law Offices of John Hauser for Defendant Cathay Motuary through Court Call.
Kevin Lee, Esq. 925-934-6102 of Van De Poel, Levy, Allen & Arnel LLP for Cross-Defendant Lewis Merlo, Inc. through Court Call.

All parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF THELMA BRUNN's Motion To Compel Deposition And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff Thelma Brunn's Motion to Compel Deposition and for Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant Cathay Mortuary-(Wah Sang) is ordered to produce its person most knowledgeable (pursuant to the categories stated in plaintiff's January 29, 2018 Amended Depo Notice for PMK at Cathan Mortuary) on June 5, 2018 at 1:30 at the Law Offices of Bruce S. Osterman, 2300 Contra Costa Blvd., Suite 320, Pleasant Hill, CA. Sanctions in the amount of $1500 are awarded against Defendant Cathay Mortuary - (Way Sang) in favor of Plaintiff Brunn. Sanctions are payable on or before June 5, 2018.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Continued To Sep-04-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 474
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, modified as to the trial date.

PLAINTIFF THELMA BRUNN's Motion To Amend Complaint Pursuant To CCP 474 is GRANTED on specified conditions which Ms. Brunn must agree to either orally at the hearing or in a writing sent to counsel for counsel for all other parties prior to the hearing. The conditions that Ms. Brunn must agree to are: 1) she must file her amendment to her complaint no later than March 29, 2018; 2) the trial is continued to September 4, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date; 2) Ms. Brunn must serve verified code-compliant responses without objection other than privilege no later than April 9, 2018 to the following deem-served discovery: a) state all facts in support of all allegations against Lewis M. Merlo, Inc.; b) state the name and contact information for each person who knows any of those facts and what facts each person knows; c) identify all documents which refer to or memorialize such facts and produce them; 3) must make all persons identified in the responses available for a deposition of no more than two hours in length no later than April 15, 2018; and 5) Lewis M. Merlo, Inc. may file a summary judgment and/or adjudication motion on 30 days' notice. If Ms. Brunn does not agree to all of these conditions, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to Lewis Mr. Merlo, Inc. by the granting of the motion.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org.
